dimensions.finding: The HLD traces executor scope, adapter usage, progress judgment, delivery checks, and reviewer handoff to canonical refs and user-confirmed sources.
Control flow covers task loading, adapter launch, initial send_input, detect_state polling, idle capture, opencalw judgment, mechanical delivery check, and reviewer handoff with failure branches.
Data flow and objects define TaskDescription, ExecutorRunState, AdapterEvidenceRecord, ProgressJudgmentResult, and MechanicalDeliveryCheckResult fields, lifecycle, and evidence movement.
Interface contracts cite source evidence and use exact invocation boundaries for Executor CLI, ClaudeTmuxAdapter, opencalw progress judgment, and reviewer handoff.
Technical decisions preserve adapter-only Claude control, opencalw-only subjective judgment, mechanical delivery checks, and reviewer-owned semantic acceptance.
Acceptance defines the executor command, real task file, preconditions, expected artifacts, mechanical checks, failure criteria, evidence capture, and human acceptance owner.
Markdown HLD visibly renders source-backed interface precision and executable acceptance design from the structured HLD, including command and expected artifact paths.
The HLD does not introduce unapproved external services, output contracts, success claims, or adapter semantics outside the contract and cited adapter sources.
The package remains HLD-only and contains no task plan, task graph, dependency edge, parallel group, or implementation task card.

overall_findings: The executor HLD is implementation-ready for the current Stage 3 test and can be consumed without untraced interface, acceptance, or completion assumptions.
